spitfire.overview: Spitfire is one of the starting heroes available to all players from the moment they begin playing Spelltroum. Despite being a starter hero, Spitfire remains competitive throughout the entire game and is capable of performing well at every skill level. Spitfire is a ranged hero that can be built in multiple ways. Players can focus on Physical Damage, Magic Damage, Attack Speed, or hybrid builds depending on their preferred playstyle. His versatility makes him one of the easiest heroes to learn while still offering plenty of depth for experienced players.
spitfire.playstyle: Spitfire is an aggressive ranged damage dealer. Because his abilities improve both his own combat performance and his teammate's effectiveness, Spitfire works well in both solo and team-oriented strategies.
strengths: Available from the start of the game
Effective with both Physical and Magic builds
Flexible and adaptable to many team compositions
weaknesses: Can be vulnerable when caught by crowd control
Can struggle against heroes that trap or isolate him
Requires proper ability timing to maximize damage
tips: Use Arrowstorm before engaging to maximize your damage output.
Fireball is excellent for farming large groups of bushes quickly.
Stay at range whenever possible.
Position carefully against melee heroes.
Coordinate Arrowstorm with teammates to maximize its Attack Speed bonus.
Save your distance advantage against heroes that rely on close combat.
Avoid getting trapped by Stonewarden's walls.

recommendedBuilds.name: Physical Damage Build
recommendedBuilds.description: Focus on Attack Speed, Critical Hits, and sustained damage output. This build turns Spitfire into a powerful ranged carry capable of overwhelming opponents with rapid attacks.

recommendedBuilds.name: Magic Damage Build
recommendedBuilds.description: Focus on Spell Power, Cooldown Reduction, and Fireball upgrades. This build emphasizes area damage and burst potential.

recommendedBuilds.name: Hybrid Build
recommendedBuilds.description: Combine Physical Damage and Magic Damage to take advantage of all parts of Spitfire's kit. This is often the most flexible approach.

synergies.freezi: Freezi is one of Spitfire's strongest partners. Both heroes are ranged damage dealers that benefit greatly from Attack Speed. When Spitfire activates Arrowstorm, both heroes gain additional Attack Speed, allowing Freezi to build up her own Attack Speed bonuses much faster. Together they can apply constant pressure while staying at a safe distance from enemies.
synergies.spiritmonger: Spirit Monger works extremely well alongside Spitfire. Spirit Monger naturally deals high damage but has fewer Attack Speed bonuses in her kit. Spitfire's Arrowstorm helps Spirit Monger attack faster, while Spirit Monger's support abilities can further enhance both heroes during team fights. Together they create a powerful ranged damage combination.
synergies.druid: Druid benefits heavily from Attack Speed buffs. When Spitfire activates Arrowstorm, both Druid and his summoned companion attack faster. This increases the team's overall damage output and helps secure objectives more quickly.
synergies.volcarn: Spitfire's Attack Speed buff is especially valuable for Volcarn. Not only does Volcarn attack faster, but his illusions also benefit from the increased Attack Speed. This can dramatically increase the amount of damage Volcarn's army deals during fights.

counters.stonewarden: Stonewarden is one of Spitfire's toughest opponents. His walls can trap Spitfire and limit his ability to reposition. Unlike some heroes, Spitfire has no built-in escape ability that can pass through walls. Stonewarden's damage reflection is also highly effective against Spitfire's rapid attacks, making prolonged fights dangerous. Because of this, Spitfire players must be careful with positioning and avoid getting trapped.

faq.q: Is Spitfire a good hero for beginners?
faq.a: Yes. Spitfire is one of the easiest heroes to learn and is available from the start of the game.

faq.q: Is Spitfire a Physical or Magic hero?
faq.a: Both. His attacks deal Physical Damage and bonus Magic Damage, allowing him to use multiple build paths effectively.
Yes. Arrowstorm can increase the Attack Speed of nearby allies, making him a valuable team fighter.

faq.q: Is Spitfire strong in late game?
faq.a: Yes. His scaling with Attack Speed, Spell Power, and his Level 10 Super Ability allows him to remain dangerous throughout the entire match.

faq.q: Should I build Physical Damage or Magic Damage?
faq.a: Both approaches are viable. The best choice depends on your preferred playstyle, your teammates, and the opponents you are facing.

faq.q: Is Spitfire weak because he is a starter hero?
faq.a: No. Starter heroes are designed to be competitive throughout the game. Spitfire remains a powerful hero even at high levels of play.
His versatility. He can farm quickly, deal strong damage, support teammates with Attack Speed buffs, and adapt to many different builds and strategies.
He lacks a reliable escape ability. If enemies manage to trap or surround him, escaping can be difficult without help from teammates or proper positioning.

abilities.BonikAutoattackDescription: Passive. Spitfire fires a flaming arrow. In addition to dealing Physical Damage, every attack also deals bonus Magic Damage. This allows Spitfire to pressure enemies even when they invest heavily into Physical Armor. His attacks naturally scale well with both Attack Speed and Spell Power builds.
abilities.BonikAttackSpeedDescription: Active Ability. Spitfire releases a flaming projectile in every direction around him. The ability also grants a powerful Attack Speed bonus. At Level 3, Arrowstorm additionally grants Attack Speed to nearby allies. This makes the ability useful both for personal damage and for supporting teammates during fights. Arrowstorm can also help clear bushes faster by hitting multiple targets simultaneously.
abilities.BonikFireballDescription: Active Ability. Spitfire launches a Fireball that explodes on impact, dealing Magic Damage in an area. Excellent for clearing bushes, farming groups of targets, and damaging multiple enemies. At Level 3, Fireball becomes significantly stronger and launches Fireballs in multiple directions at once, allowing Spitfire to clear large portions of the battlefield extremely quickly.

viverna.overview: Viverna is an aggressive ranged mage that specializes in harassment, mobility, and constant pressure. Unlike heroes that rely on long fights, Viverna excels at quickly engaging, dealing damage, and escaping before enemies can retaliate. Her poison attacks, short cooldowns, and powerful mobility make her one of the most dangerous heroes for controlling the pace of a match. Viverna is especially effective against slow or predictable opponents, constantly forcing them to react while she remains difficult to catch.
viverna.playstyle: Viverna thrives on aggressive hit-and-run gameplay. A skilled Viverna player is rarely standing still — she attacks, repositions, and strikes again before the enemy has a chance to respond.
strengths: Effective throughout all stages of a match
weaknesses: Can be punished if Blink is unavailable
Struggles when forced into extended close-range fights
tips: Use Poison to harass enemies before fully committing to a fight.
Blink can be used both offensively and defensively.
Cross Blast is excellent for farming and combat.
Avoid extended fights against tanky heroes.
Always keep an escape route in mind.
Use mobility to control the pace of engagements.
Let Poison continue dealing damage while you reposition.

synergies.faerie: Fairy and Viverna form one of the strongest mage combinations in Spelltroum. Both heroes excel at mobility, ranged pressure, and avoiding direct confrontations. Together they can continuously harass opponents, deal Magic Damage, and retreat before enemies can respond.
synergies.druid: Druid complements Viverna's playstyle extremely well. His Speed Aura helps both heroes remain mobile while his summoned companion applies additional pressure. This combination excels at controlling the map and forcing enemies into unfavorable fights.
synergies.spiritmonger: Spiritmonger's movement buffs allow both heroes to engage and disengage even more effectively. Together they become extremely difficult to catch while maintaining constant pressure on opponents.
synergies.grizzly: Although their playstyles are very different, Grizzly and Viverna work surprisingly well together. Grizzly serves as a durable frontline while Viverna attacks from a safe distance. Enemies often focus on Grizzly, giving Viverna opportunities to freely apply Poison and Cross Blast damage.

counters.grizzly: Grizzly is one of Viverna's toughest opponents. His Blink allows him to follow Viverna even after she escapes. Once Grizzly reaches melee range and applies his slow, Viverna can quickly find herself trapped in an unfavorable fight. His large Health pool also makes it difficult for Viverna to burst him down.
counters.freezi: Freezi's crowd control is extremely dangerous for Viverna. A successful stun can leave Viverna vulnerable long enough for Freezi to deal lethal damage. Because Viverna relies heavily on mobility, losing control of her movement is especially punishing.
counters.death-slayer: Death Slayer's stun and aggressive engage tools make him a constant threat. If he manages to catch Viverna, she often has very little time to react. Viverna players must carefully manage their positioning and keep escape options available at all times.

faq.a: Viverna is relatively easy to learn but difficult to master. New players can quickly understand her abilities, but maximizing her mobility requires practice.
Yes. Most of Viverna's damage comes from Magic Damage abilities and Spell Power scaling.
Her mobility. Blink allows Viverna to engage, escape, and reposition better than most heroes.
Crowd control. If Viverna is stunned or locked down, she becomes much easier to eliminate.

faq.q: Which heroes work best with Viverna?
faq.a: Fairy, Druid, Spirit Monger, and Grizzly all provide strong synergies with Viverna's aggressive and mobile playstyle.
Grizzly, Freezi, and Death Slayer are among her most difficult matchups due to their ability to catch or disable her.

faq.q: Is Viverna strong in late game?
faq.a: Yes. Her mobility, short cooldowns, and powerful Super Ability allow her to remain a dangerous threat throughout the entire match.

abilities.QuopAutoattackDescription: Basic Attack. Viverna's attacks poison enemies. Poison deals damage over time for 4 seconds, allowing Viverna to damage opponents even after disengaging. This makes every successful attack valuable and forces enemies to either retreat or continue fighting while taking damage. Poison also prevents enemies from comfortably regenerating Health between trades.
abilities.QuopBlinkDescription: Active Ability. Viverna instantly teleports a short distance. Blink can be used to engage enemies, escape dangerous situations, dodge projectiles, reposition during fights, and cross obstacles quickly. This ability gives Viverna exceptional mobility and makes her one of the hardest heroes in Spelltroum to pin down.
abilities.QuopDamageDescription: Active Ability. Viverna releases a magical explosion around herself that deals Magic Damage to nearby enemies. Cross Blast has an extremely short cooldown and serves as Viverna's primary damage ability. Effective for farming bushes, clearing neutral creeps, harassing enemies, and finishing low-health targets. At Level 3, reaches only a 3-second cooldown, allowing Viverna to continuously pressure opponents.

death-slayer.overview: Death Slayer is a melee fighter that can be played as a carry, a support, or a crowd-control specialist. He combines Critical Chance, Lifesteal Aura, and stun control, making him extremely dangerous in direct fights when he gets close to enemies. Death Slayer can become very strong if he farms well, but he is harder for beginners because he is a melee hero. He needs good positioning, enough survivability, and the right timing to engage.
death-slayer.playstyle: Death Slayer excels in prolonged close-range fights. His strength comes from surviving long enough to keep attacking and healing. Depending on how he is played, Death Slayer can become a Lifesteal carry, a durable support, a stun-focused controller, or a melee bruiser.
playstyleHighlights: Scale into a strong late-game fighter
strengths: Additional Critical Chance on basic attacks
Provides Lifesteal Aura to himself and allies
Can be played in multiple styles
Very strong if he farms well
weaknesses: Harder for beginners than ranged heroes
Needs good positioning to reach enemies
Lifesteal does not work on bushes
tips: Use Stun to catch enemies before engaging.
Do not chase ranged heroes without a plan.
Farm efficiently before forcing major fights.
Stay near allies so they benefit from Lifesteal Aura.
Lifesteal works on heroes and creeps, but not on bushes.
Cooldown Reduction can support stun-focused builds.
Death Slayer becomes much stronger when he gets enough survivability.

synergies.freezi: Freezi is one of Death Slayer's strongest partners. Freezi provides bonus damage through her aura, while Death Slayer provides Lifesteal through his aura. Together, they make each other much stronger in fights. Freezi can also help control enemies, giving Death Slayer more time to reach and attack them.
synergies.druid: Druid's Speed Aura helps Death Slayer overcome one of his biggest weaknesses: mobility. In return, Death Slayer gives Druid and his summoned companion Lifesteal. This creates a strong team that can move quickly, fight longer, and recover Health during combat.
synergies.spiritmonger: Spirit Monger benefits greatly from Death Slayer's Lifesteal Aura because she can deal high damage. Death Slayer also benefits from Spirit Monger's movement support, making it easier for him to engage or escape. Together, they create a strong aggressive duo.
synergies.spitfire: Spitfire's Attack Speed buff works well with Death Slayer. More Attack Speed means more attacks, more Critical Hits, and more Lifesteal. At the same time, Spitfire benefits from Death Slayer's Lifesteal Aura during fights.

counters.volcarn: Volcarn can be uncomfortable for Death Slayer to fight. His illusions create multiple targets, making it harder for Death Slayer to focus the real enemy. Death Slayer prefers direct duels, while Volcarn makes fights chaotic and harder to control.
counters.viverna: Viverna can heavily pressure Death Slayer, especially early in the match. Her poison, mobility, and hit-and-run playstyle make it difficult for Death Slayer to farm safely or force direct fights. If Viverna prevents him from farming, Death Slayer may struggle to reach his strongest point.
counters.grizzly: Grizzly can be difficult for Death Slayer because he also wants close-range fights. If Grizzly builds enough damage and survivability, he can sometimes overpower Death Slayer in direct combat. This matchup often depends heavily on items, levels, and timing.

faq.q: Is Death Slayer good for beginners?
faq.a: He is playable for beginners, but harder than ranged heroes. Because he is melee, he needs better positioning and timing to avoid being kited or punished early.

faq.q: Is Death Slayer a carry or a support?
faq.a: He can be both. Death Slayer can be played as a Lifesteal carry, but his Lifesteal Aura also makes him useful as a supportive frontliner.

faq.q: What is Death Slayer's biggest strength?
faq.a: His sustain. With Lifesteal Aura and the right items, Death Slayer can survive long fights and recover a large amount of Health while attacking.

faq.q: What is Death Slayer's biggest weakness?
faq.a: His melee range. If enemies keep distance, harass him, or prevent him from farming, he can struggle to reach his full strength.
No. Lifesteal only works against enemy heroes and neutral creeps.

faq.q: Which heroes work best with Death Slayer?
faq.a: Freezi, Druid, Spirit Monger, and Spitfire all work well with Death Slayer because they benefit from his Lifesteal Aura or help him reach enemies.
Viverna, Vulkarn, and Grizzly can be difficult matchups depending on the situation.

faq.q: Is Death Slayer strong in late game?
faq.a: Yes. If Death Slayer farms well, he can become one of the strongest close-range fighters in Spelltroum.

abilities.SkeletAutoattackDescription: Passive. Death Slayer's basic attacks have additional Critical Chance. This makes his attacks more dangerous than they may seem, especially when combined with critical damage or lifesteal items. Because he is a melee hero, he needs to reach enemies before he can fully use this strength.
abilities.SkeletStunDescription: Active Ability. Death Slayer releases stunning projectiles in all directions. Enemies hit by these projectiles take damage and become stunned for a short duration. This ability can be used to catch enemies, interrupt attacks, escape dangerous situations, protect allies, and start fights. A well-timed Stun can completely change the outcome of a fight.
abilities.SkeletLifestealDescription: Passive Aura. Death Slayer provides Lifesteal to himself and nearby allies. This aura allows affected heroes to restore Health when damaging enemy heroes or neutral creeps. Lifesteal does not work on bushes. Because Lifesteal effects stack, Death Slayer can become extremely difficult to kill when combined with Lifesteal items or heroes that benefit from sustained combat.

druid.overview: Druid is a highly mobile summoner focused on farming, map control, and strategic positioning rather than direct combat. Unlike many heroes, Druid does not want to enter prolonged face-to-face fights. Instead, he uses his movement speed, summoned creeps, and unique bush mechanics to outfarm opponents, collect Crowns, and create favorable engagements. His playstyle revolves around controlling the battlefield, creating pressure through summoned units, and constantly moving around the map. Druid is primarily a Physical Damage hero.
druid.playstyle: A good Druid player constantly moves around the map instead of looking for direct fights. His goal is to farm faster than opponents, reach important item upgrades early, force enemies into bad positions, and use summoned creeps to create numerical advantages.
strengths: Can create favorable fights through positioning
weaknesses: Summons can be countered by area damage
Less effective when forced into straight-up fights
tips: Focus on farming rather than forcing fights.
Use bushes to control enemy movement.
Summon creeps in aggressive positions.
Speed Aura is valuable throughout the entire match.
Create favorable fights instead of fair fights.
Use your mobility advantage to collect Crowns quickly.
Your creeps are strongest when fighting alongside you.

synergies.freezi: Freezi is one of Druid's strongest partners. Freezi provides a Damage Aura that benefits both Druid and his summoned creeps. Meanwhile, Druid's Speed Aura helps Freezi reposition and farm more efficiently. Together they become extremely effective at controlling the map.
synergies.death-slayer: Death Slayer's Lifesteal Aura works on both Druid and his summoned creeps. At the same time, Druid's Speed Aura helps Death Slayer reach enemies more easily. This creates a strong combination of sustain and mobility.
synergies.stonewarden: Stonewarden provides Armor Aura while Druid provides Speed Aura. In addition, Druid can summon creeps from bushes or walls created during fights, creating interesting opportunities for battlefield control. Together they form a durable and difficult-to-engage team.
synergies.spiritmonger: Druid's bush creation works extremely well with Spirit Monger. When Druid creates bushes behind enemies, Spirit Monger's Orb can bounce between the enemy and the newly created bush. This can result in additional orb hits and significantly increased damage.
synergies.volcarn: In late-game matches, Druid and Volcarn can overwhelm opponents with numbers. Druid summons creeps while Volcarn creates illusions. Combined with Speed Aura, the battlefield can quickly become filled with allied units that pressure enemies from multiple directions.

counters.spiritmonger: Spirit Monger is one of Druid's most difficult matchups. The bushes Druid creates can actually help Spirit Monger. Her Orb can bounce between Druid and the newly created bushes, allowing her to deal significantly more damage than normal. This often turns Druid's own mechanic against him.
counters.viverna: Viverna is difficult for Druid because she avoids direct engagements. She can poison Druid, blink away, and continuously harass him without allowing him to fully utilize his trapping mechanics. Her mobility makes it difficult for Druid to lock her down.
counters.faerie: Faerie can escape many of Druid's strongest setups. Even if Druid traps her with bushes and summons a creep behind her, Fairy can simply fly through enemies and obstacles. This makes many of Druid's positioning advantages less effective.
counters.death-slayer: While not a direct counter, a heavily farmed Death Slayer can become problematic. Death Slayer can heal from Druid's summoned creeps through Lifesteal, turning Druid's own summons into a source of healing. This can become dangerous during longer fights.

faq.q: Is Druid a damage dealer or a support?
faq.a: Both. Druid deals respectable damage through his creeps and attacks, but he also provides valuable utility through Speed Aura.
He is easy to learn but difficult to master. Success with Druid depends heavily on positioning, map awareness, and efficient farming.
Yes. Speed Aura increases the movement speed of Druid and nearby allies.
Yes. His Basic Attack has a chance to create bushes near enemies, which can then be transformed into summoned creeps.
His ability to control the map and generate advantages through mobility and summoned units.
Direct combat. Druid generally prefers smart positioning and favorable engagements rather than straight-up fights.

faq.q: Which heroes work best with Druid?
faq.a: Freezi, Death Slayer, Stonewarden, Spirit Monger, and Volcarn all have strong synergy with Druid.
Spirit Monger, Viverna, and Fairy are among the most difficult heroes for Druid to face.

abilities.FutionAutoattackDescription: Passive. Druid throws an enchanted sickle. Each attack has a 50% chance to deal additional damage and create a bush behind the target based on the projectile direction. If an enemy is fighting near walls or obstacles, the newly created bush can block escape routes and trap them. The bush can also immediately be transformed into a summoned creep, allowing Druid to suddenly create a 2-versus-1 fight.
abilities.FutionCreepDescription: Active Ability. Druid transforms a bush into an allied monster. The summoned creep fights alongside Druid and can help farm bushes, pressure enemy heroes, block movement, and secure kills. Because Druid can create bushes himself, he can often summon creeps exactly where he wants them. A well-placed creep can completely change the outcome of a fight.
abilities.FutionSpeedDescription: Passive Aura. Druid constantly increases the movement speed of himself and nearby allies, including summoned creeps. The aura allows Druid and his team to rotate faster, escape dangerous situations, chase enemies more effectively, and farm the map more efficiently. Speed Aura is one of the strongest utility abilities in Spelltroum because movement speed is useful during every stage of the match.

freezi.overview: Freezi is a powerful ranged carry hero that becomes increasingly dangerous as the match progresses. She combines scaling Attack Speed, crowd control, and a powerful Damage Aura, allowing her to deal tremendous damage while also strengthening her team. While Freezi performs reasonably well during the early game, she truly shines in the late game when her Attack Speed ramps up and her Frost Strike reaches maximum level. A well-farmed Freezi can quickly eliminate enemies before they have a chance to react.
freezi.playstyle: Unlike some heroes that rely on mobility, Freezi wins through positioning, damage output, and crowd control. Her goal is to farm efficiently, upgrade Frost Strike, build enough survivability to avoid being burst down, and eliminate enemies before they can escape.
strengths: Dangerous in both duels and team fights
weaknesses: Vulnerable if caught by enemy crowd control
Requires farming to reach full potential
Can struggle against highly mobile heroes
tips: Try to maintain continuous attacks on the same target.
Use Frost Strike before committing to a fight.
Position carefully behind allies whenever possible.
Freezi becomes significantly stronger as the match progresses.
Protect yourself from enemy crowd control.
Your Damage Aura helps allies even when you are not actively attacking.
Late-game Ricochet attacks can dramatically increase your damage output.

synergies.death-slayer: One of Freezi's strongest partnerships. Freezi provides Damage Aura, Death Slayer provides Lifesteal Aura. Together they create an incredibly powerful combination of damage and sustain. Freezi hits harder while Death Slayer helps both heroes survive longer.
synergies.spitfire: Spitfire's Attack Speed buff combines perfectly with Freezi's scaling Attack Speed. Freezi provides bonus damage while Spitfire increases attack frequency. Together they can overwhelm opponents with ranged damage.
synergies.druid: Druid's Speed Aura helps Freezi reposition and kite enemies. Meanwhile, Freezi's Damage Aura benefits both Druid and his summoned creeps. This combination creates a highly mobile team capable of controlling large areas of the map.
synergies.spiritmonger: Spirit Monger benefits greatly from Freezi's Damage Aura. Together they create a dangerous ranged composition that combines burst damage, crowd control, and scaling damage output.

counters.stonewarden: Stonewarden is one of Freezi's most difficult opponents. His walls can block movement and create unfavorable positions for Freezi. In addition, his damage reflection punishes Freezi for continuously attacking. Because Freezi relies heavily on repeated attacks, Stonewarden can be extremely frustrating to fight.
counters.viverna: Viverna's mobility can make it difficult for Freezi to consistently land damage. If Viverna avoids Frost Strike and continuously harasses from range, Freezi can struggle to build momentum.
counters.grizzly: A farmed Grizzly can become a serious threat. If Grizzly successfully reaches melee range, his durability and damage can overwhelm Freezi before she has enough time to eliminate him.

faq.a: Yes. Freezi is primarily designed as a ranged carry that becomes stronger throughout the match.
Not completely. Her abilities are easy to understand, but proper positioning and target selection require experience.
Her ability to rapidly eliminate targets once she starts attacking uninterrupted.
Getting caught before she can deal damage. Freezi relies on positioning and protection from allies.

faq.q: Why is Frost Strike so powerful?
faq.a: At maximum level, Frost Strike can stun enemies for 2 seconds, creating a huge opportunity to secure kills.

faq.q: Which heroes work best with Freezi?
faq.a: Death Slayer, Spitfire, Druid, and Spirit Monger all have excellent synergy with Freezi.
Stonewarden is one of her strongest counters, while highly mobile heroes such as Viverna can also create problems.

faq.q: Is Freezi strong in late game?
faq.a: Absolutely. Freezi is one of the strongest late-game carries in Spelltroum thanks to her scaling Attack Speed, Damage Aura, and Ricochet Shot.

abilities.FriziAutoattackDescription: Passive. Freezi fires an Ice Arrow. Each successful attack increases her Attack Speed against that target. The longer she is allowed to continuously attack, the faster she becomes. This makes Freezi extremely dangerous during extended engagements and one of the strongest single-target damage dealers in Spelltroum.
abilities.FriziStunDescription: Active Ability. Freezi throws an Ice Crystal that explodes on impact. Enemies hit by the explosion take damage and become stunned. At Level 1, Frost Strike provides a short stun. At Level 3, the stun duration increases to 2 seconds — often enough for Freezi to completely eliminate fragile targets before they can react. Frost Strike is one of the strongest crowd-control abilities in the game.
abilities.FriziDamageDescription: Passive Aura. Freezi constantly increases the Attack Damage of herself and nearby allies. Freezi receives the largest benefit from the aura, but all nearby teammates gain bonus damage as well. This makes her valuable not only as a carry but also as a strong team-oriented hero. The aura scales especially well with heroes that attack quickly or rely heavily on Physical Damage.

flameweaver.overview: Flameweaver is a unique ranged mage whose abilities deal True Damage. Unlike most magic-based heroes, Flameweaver's abilities are not affected by Magic Critical Chance, Magic Resistance reduction, or other traditional magic damage amplifiers. Instead, his damage ignores both Physical Armor and Magic Resistance, making him extremely effective against heavily armored targets. Flameweaver is one of the most mechanically challenging heroes in Spelltroum. Players who master his portals, mana management, and positioning can become incredibly difficult to deal with.
flameweaver.playstyle: Unlike many carries, Flameweaver's power comes from resource management. The more mana he has, the stronger his Basic Attack becomes. A skilled Flameweaver constantly balances mana usage and damage output while using Portals to stay mobile and unpredictable.
weaknesses: Can become weak if starved of Mana
Vulnerable when behind in gold and experience
tips: Avoid wasting Mana unnecessarily.
Your Basic Attack becomes stronger when your Mana pool is full.
Use Path of Fire to control enemy movement rather than simply dealing damage.
Portals can be used both offensively and defensively.
Heavily armored enemies are ideal targets.
Positioning is more important than raw damage output.
Think ahead when placing portals.

synergies.spitfire: Spitfire's Attack Speed buff works exceptionally well with Flameweaver. More Attack Speed means more Mana-scaled attacks and higher overall damage output. Together they create a powerful offensive duo.
synergies.spiritmonger: Spirit Monger's movement bonuses help Flameweaver reposition more effectively. The added mobility allows him to place Portals and Path of Fire more aggressively. Together they become extremely difficult to catch.
synergies.druid: Druid's Speed Aura complements Flameweaver's mobile playstyle. The additional movement speed allows Flameweaver to reposition quickly and make better use of his area-control abilities.
synergies.freezi: Freezi's Damage Aura increases the effectiveness of Flameweaver's attacks while her crowd control makes it easier for him to place Path of Fire in optimal positions.

counters.grizzly: Grizzly can be difficult for Flameweaver to handle. His enormous Health pool allows him to survive long enough to reach melee range. Once Grizzly closes the gap, Flameweaver can struggle to escape sustained pressure.
counters.viverna: Viverna's mobility and hit-and-run playstyle make her a frustrating opponent. She can poison Flameweaver, disengage quickly, and avoid standing in Path of Fire. This often forces Flameweaver into unfavorable situations.

faq.a: Yes, but he is a very unusual mage. His abilities deal True Damage instead of traditional Magic Damage.

faq.q: Does Magic Critical Chance affect Flameweaver?
faq.a: No. True Damage cannot critically strike through Magic Critical Chance bonuses.

faq.q: Does Magic Resistance reduction increase Flameweaver's damage?
faq.a: No. True Damage ignores both Magic Resistance and Physical Armor.

faq.q: Why is Mana important for Flameweaver?
faq.a: His Basic Attack scales with his current Mana. The more Mana he has, the more damage he deals.
His ability to deal reliable True Damage regardless of enemy defenses.
Mana dependency. Poor Mana management significantly reduces his effectiveness.

faq.q: Which heroes work best with Flameweaver?
faq.a: Spitfire, Spirit Monger, Druid, and Freezi all provide strong synergy with Flameweaver.
Grizzly, Viverna, and heroes that heavily pressure him during the early game can create difficult matchups.
Yes. Because his damage ignores armor and resistance, he remains effective against heavily defensive targets and PvE bosses with high defenses.

abilities.WarlockBasicAttackDescription: Passive. Flameweaver's Basic Attack deals additional damage based on his current Mana. The more Mana he has available, the stronger his attacks become. This creates a unique playstyle where Mana acts as both a resource and a damage amplifier. Because the bonus damage is based on current Mana, Flameweaver becomes significantly weaker if he spends too much Mana carelessly. Proper Mana management is one of the keys to mastering the hero.
abilities.WarlockFireLineDescription: Active Ability. Flameweaver creates a line of fire in front of him. The fire remains on the battlefield for several seconds. Enemies standing in the flames take periodic True Damage. Path of Fire can be used to control space, block enemy movement, defend objectives, escape pursuers, and force enemies into unfavorable positions. Heroes that rely on direct melee engagements often struggle to fight through the fire.
abilities.WarlockHideDescription: Active Ability. Flameweaver creates a pair of connected portals. Any unit that enters one portal instantly exits through the other, providing incredible mobility and allowing unexpected plays. Enemies that travel through the portals lose Mana — stolen Mana is transferred to Flameweaver. This allows him to sustain his Mana pool, increase his Basic Attack damage, and punish Mana-dependent heroes. Portal management is one of the most skill-intensive mechanics in Spelltroum.

shadow-fury.overview: Shadow Fury is a highly mobile physical assassin that specializes in burst damage, ambushes, and hit-and-run tactics. Unlike most heroes in Spelltroum, all of Shadow Fury's abilities deal Physical Damage. Because of this, Shadow Fury benefits greatly from Physical Critical Chance, Physical Critical Damage, and Physical Armor Reduction — while gaining very little value from magic-based stats. A skilled Shadow Fury player rarely fights enemies head-on. Instead, she attacks from unexpected angles, deals massive burst damage, and escapes before enemies can retaliate.
shadow-fury.playstyle: Shadow Fury is one of the most difficult heroes in Spelltroum, but also one of the most rewarding when mastered. Her goal is to farm safely, build Physical Damage and Critical Strike, find isolated targets, strike first, and escape before enemies can respond.
strengths: Strong scaling with Physical Critical builds
Difficult to catch when played correctly
weaknesses: Fragile when Orb Shield is unavailable
tips: Avoid direct fights whenever possible.
Always look for isolated targets.
Physical Critical builds are usually your strongest option.
Use Orb Shield before entering dangerous situations.
Don't waste Shadow Cloak unnecessarily.
Prioritize enemies with low armor.
Positioning is more important than raw damage.

synergies.viverna: Viverna and Shadow Fury create an extremely mobile team. Both heroes prefer hit-and-run combat instead of direct fights. They constantly pressure enemies, force mistakes, and disengage before opponents can retaliate. Both heroes are difficult to catch and can quickly overwhelm isolated targets.
synergies.death-slayer: Death Slayer's Lifesteal Aura works exceptionally well with Shadow Fury's Physical Damage. The additional sustain allows Shadow Fury to recover Health while attacking and remain active on the map longer. Death Slayer's stun also creates opportunities for Shadow Fury to safely land empowered attacks.
synergies.spiritmonger: Spirit Monger's movement support helps Shadow Fury engage and disengage more effectively. The additional mobility makes it easier for Shadow Fury to find favorable fights and avoid dangerous situations.
synergies.druid: Druid's Speed Aura allows Shadow Fury to move around the map faster and hunt isolated targets more efficiently. Together they create a highly mobile team composition.

counters.viverna: Although they can work well together as allies, Viverna is also one of Shadow Fury's most annoying opponents. Her Poison continuously damages Shadow Fury. Because Orb Shield loses charges whenever it takes damage, a single poison attack can quickly remove the shield's protection.
counters.stonewarden: Stonewarden is one of Shadow Fury's hardest counters. His Armor Aura reduces Shadow Fury's Physical Damage. His damage reflection can remove Orb Shield charges. His naturally high armor makes him extremely difficult to burst down.
counters.flameweaver: Flameweaver's fire continuously deals damage over time. The periodic damage quickly removes Orb Shield charges and makes it difficult for Shadow Fury to engage safely. Many Flameweaver builds also use Mana Shield, making him surprisingly durable against burst damage.

faq.a: No. Despite using abilities, all of Shadow Fury's damage is Physical Damage.

faq.q: Does Magic Critical Chance help Shadow Fury?
faq.a: No. None of Shadow Fury's abilities benefit from Magic Critical Chance.

faq.q: Does Magic Armor Reduction help Shadow Fury?
faq.a: No. Shadow Fury primarily benefits from Physical Armor Reduction.

faq.q: What is Shadow Fury's biggest strength?
faq.a: Her ability to quickly eliminate enemy heroes before they can react.

faq.q: What is Shadow Fury's biggest weakness?
faq.a: Armor and damage-over-time effects. Both significantly reduce her effectiveness.
No. Shadow Fury is one of the more difficult heroes in Spelltroum and requires strong positioning and decision-making.

faq.q: Which heroes work best with Shadow Fury?
faq.a: Viverna, Death Slayer, Spirit Monger, and Druid all provide strong synergy with Shadow Fury's mobile playstyle.
Stonewarden, Flameweaver, and Viverna are among the most challenging matchups.

faq.q: How should I build Shadow Fury?
faq.a: Focus on Physical Damage, Physical Critical Chance, and Critical Damage. Avoid Magic Critical and Magic Damage-focused items.

abilities.ShadowAutoattackDescription: Passive. Shadow Fury fires empowered bolts that deal increased Physical Damage. Each attack also reduces the target's Physical Armor, making subsequent hits deal more damage. Her attacks scale exceptionally well with Physical Damage, Critical Chance, and Critical Damage bonuses. Because all of her damage is physical, item selection is extremely important.
abilities.ShadowDamageProtectionDescription: Active Ability. Shadow Fury creates a protective shield that blocks 100% of incoming damage. Each blocked attack or damaging ability consumes one shield charge. At Level 1, Orb Shield blocks a single hit. At Level 3, Orb Shield can block up to three hits. After Orb Shield activates, Shadow Fury gains a temporary Movement Speed boost, allowing her to reposition or escape danger.
abilities.ShadowHiddenAttackDescription: Active Ability. Shadow Fury becomes invisible for a short duration. While invisible, she prepares a devastating empowered attack. The first attack after leaving invisibility deals additional damage based on the target's current Health — the healthier the target, the more bonus damage they receive. This makes Shadow Fury especially effective against heroes with large Health pools but low armor. Shadow Cloak is the foundation of Shadow Fury's assassin playstyle.

spiritmonger.overview: Spiritmonger is one of the most versatile heroes in Spelltroum. She combines strong Physical Damage, powerful Magic Damage, excellent mobility, and strong team utility. Because of this, she can be played as both a carry and a support-oriented damage dealer. Spiritmonger rewards players who enjoy heroes that can adapt to different situations. Unlike many heroes that focus on either Physical or Magical Damage, Spiritmonger successfully combines both.
spiritmonger.playstyle: Her gameplay revolves around collecting Souls and using them to increase her attack damage. As Spiritmonger gathers Souls, her Basic Attack becomes increasingly dangerous. A well-farmed Spiritmonger can become one of the highest damage dealers in the game. At the same time, her Soul Orb gives her powerful magical burst damage, while Velocity provides exceptional mobility for both herself and her allies.
strengths: Effective throughout all stages of a match
Can be played aggressively or defensively
One of the best scaling heroes in Spelltroum
weaknesses: Loses Souls on death before Level 10
Relies on farming to reach maximum potential
Can struggle against tanky melee heroes
Soul Orb requires practice to use effectively
tips: Farm Souls whenever possible.
Avoid unnecessary deaths before reaching Level 10.
Use bushes to maximize Soul Orb bounces.
Save Velocity for important moments.
Position carefully during team fights.
Use your mobility advantage to control engagements.
Remember that a fully stacked Spiritmonger becomes a powerful carry.

synergies.spitfire: Spitfire provides Attack Speed through Arrowstorm. This allows Spiritmonger to attack much faster and take full advantage of her accumulated Soul Damage. Together they create a powerful ranged damage composition.
synergies.stonewarden: Stonewarden creates one of Spiritmonger's strongest combinations. When Stonewarden traps an enemy near a wall, Soul Orb can repeatedly bounce between the enemy and the wall. Stonewarden's Armor Aura also helps Spiritmonger survive longer during fights.
synergies.druid: Druid's bushes create additional bounce opportunities for Soul Orb. When Druid places a bush near an enemy, Spiritmonger's orb can bounce between the enemy and the bush, often hitting the target multiple times. This is one of the strongest synergy combinations in the game.
synergies.death-slayer: Death Slayer's Lifesteal Aura provides valuable sustain for Spiritmonger. The additional survivability allows her to remain aggressive while continuing to farm Souls and pressure enemies.
synergies.freezi: Freezi's crowd control gives Spiritmonger time to land Soul Orb more reliably. Meanwhile, Freezi's Damage Aura increases the effectiveness of Spiritmonger's attacks.

counters.shadow-fury: Shadow Fury can be frustrating to play against. Orb Shield can completely block Soul Orb damage, removing one of Spiritmonger's strongest offensive tools. At the same time, Shadow Fury can quickly strike and retreat before Spiritmonger has time to respond.
counters.grizzly: Grizzly's massive Health pool makes him difficult to burst down. If he reaches Spiritmonger in close combat, she can quickly find herself overwhelmed. Spiritmonger generally prefers fighting from range rather than standing toe-to-toe with tanky heroes.
counters.death-slayer: Death Slayer is a dangerous opponent once he reaches melee range. His stun allows him to close the distance, and his Lifesteal makes extended fights difficult. If Spiritmonger cannot keep space between them, Death Slayer can become a serious threat.

faq.a: Each collected Soul grants +10 Attack Damage.

faq.q: Can Spiritmonger collect Souls from bushes?
faq.a: Yes. Souls can be collected from defeated bushes, creeps, and enemy heroes.
Normally, she loses half of her collected Souls.

faq.q: How do I stop losing Souls?
faq.a: Unlock the Super Ability by upgrading Spiritmonger to Hero Power 9 and reach Level 10 during a match.

faq.q: Is Spiritmonger a carry or a support?
faq.a: Both. She can scale into a powerful carry while also providing valuable team utility through Velocity.
Soul Orb is generally considered her most impactful damage ability because of its bouncing mechanic and high burst potential.

faq.q: Which heroes work best with Spiritmonger?
faq.a: Spitfire, Stonewarden, Druid, Death Slayer, and Freezi all have excellent synergy with Spiritmonger.
Shadow Fury, Grizzly, and Death Slayer can all create difficult matchups depending on the situation.
Yes. She has an accessible kit, strong mobility, and multiple ways to contribute to a match, while still offering a high skill ceiling for experienced players.

abilities.SpiritmongerAutoattackDescription: Basic Attack. Spiritmonger throws an enchanted fan that collects Souls from defeated enemies, creeps, and bushes. Each Soul grants +10 Attack Damage. The more Souls she collects, the stronger her Basic Attack becomes. When Spiritmonger dies, she normally loses half of her collected Souls — creating a balance between aggressive farming and staying alive.
abilities.SpiritmongerDamageDescription: Active Ability. Spiritmonger launches a magical orb that bounces between targets — enemy heroes, bushes, and other valid targets. Enemies positioned near bushes can be hit multiple times by the same orb. A properly placed Soul Orb can deal massive damage and is one of Spiritmonger's strongest abilities. The orb becomes especially dangerous in tight areas where enemies can become trapped between obstacles.
abilities.SpiritmongerSpeedDescription: Active Ability. Spiritmonger gains increased Movement Speed for a short duration. At Level 3, Velocity also grants Movement Speed to nearby allies, making Spiritmonger one of the strongest mobility supports in Spelltroum. Can be used to chase enemies, escape dangerous situations, reposition during fights, or help teammates engage and retreat.

mortifier.overview: Mortifier is one of the most unique heroes in Spelltroum. He combines Physical Damage, Magical Damage, crowd control, positioning tricks, and explosive burst damage into a single hero. Unlike most melee heroes, Mortifier can be played in several completely different ways. Some players build him as a durable melee carry that relies on his powerful double attacks. Others focus entirely on his bombs and play him as a burst mage capable of instantly eliminating enemies with clever trap setups. Because of his versatility, Mortifier rewards creativity and game knowledge more than almost any other hero.
mortifier.playstyle: Unlike straightforward heroes, Mortifier is constantly looking for opportunities to outplay opponents. A skilled Mortifier player can turn a losing fight into a winning one with a single well-timed Swap. His signature combo: place Blast Traps, wait for an enemy to approach, then Swap them directly onto the traps — multiple explosions at once.
playstyleHighlights: Playing as either Physical Damage carry or Magical Damage caster
strengths: Can be played as Physical Damage or Magical Damage
weaknesses: Can struggle against very tanky heroes
Some heroes can completely negate his trap combos
tips: Don't rely only on traps.
Use Swap creatively.
Save teammates when possible.
Hide trap setups in unexpected locations.
Build Physical Damage if you want stronger melee combat.
Build Magical Damage if you want stronger trap combos.
Remember that enemies can see your traps.
Sometimes the threat of a trap is stronger than the trap itself.

synergies.viverna: Viverna and Mortifier create a highly aggressive duo. Both heroes excel at hit-and-run gameplay and can quickly overwhelm enemies with burst damage. Viverna pressures enemies from range while Mortifier looks for opportunities to land a devastating Swap combo.
synergies.spiritmonger: Spiritmonger's Velocity provides valuable movement speed. This allows Mortifier to reach targets faster, reposition more easily, set up trap combos more reliably, and escape dangerous situations. The increased mobility greatly improves Mortifier's effectiveness.
synergies.druid: Druid's Speed Aura helps Mortifier move around the map more efficiently. The additional movement speed makes it easier to chase enemies and execute Swap plays.
synergies.freezi: Freezi's stun creates perfect opportunities for Mortifier. A stunned enemy has much less time to react to incoming traps or Swap combinations. Together they can create devastating crowd-control chains.

counters.grizzly: Grizzly is one of Mortifier's toughest opponents. His enormous Health pool makes it difficult to eliminate him with trap combinations. Even when hit by multiple bombs, Grizzly often survives long enough to continue fighting.
counters.shadow-fury: Shadow Fury can completely disrupt Mortifier's burst combos. If she activates Orb Shield before the explosions connect, the damage can be absorbed entirely. A failed combo often leaves Mortifier vulnerable to a counterattack.
counters.viverna: Viverna's mobility makes her difficult to catch. Her Blink allows her to avoid trap zones and reposition quickly. Mortifier often struggles to force favorable engagements against skilled Viverna players.

faq.q: Is Mortifier a carry or a mage?
faq.a: Both. Mortifier can be built as a Physical Damage carry focused on double attacks or as a Magical Damage hero focused on Blast Trap combos.
Yes. Swap can be used on both allies and enemies.
No. Blast Traps remain active until they explode or expire naturally.
The classic Blast Trap + Swap combo is generally considered his strongest play.
Not really. His abilities are simple to understand but difficult to master. Most of his power comes from positioning, timing, and creative use of Swap.

faq.q: What happens when Mortifier dies at Level 10?
faq.a: If his Super Ability is unlocked, he automatically leaves behind a Blast Trap at the location where he died.

faq.q: Which heroes work best with Mortifier?
faq.a: Viverna, Spiritmonger, Druid, and Freezi all provide strong synergy with Mortifier.
Grizzly, Shadow Fury, and highly mobile heroes are generally the most difficult opponents for Mortifier.

abilities.MortifierAutoattackDescription: Basic Attack. Mortifier's attacks have a chance to instantly reset their cooldown and strike again. The second attack deals additional Physical Damage and restores Health to Mortifier. Because of this mechanic, Mortifier can become surprisingly dangerous in direct melee combat. When built as a Physical Damage hero, repeated double attacks allow him to deal massive sustained damage while healing himself at the same time.
abilities.MortifierSwapDescription: Active Ability. Mortifier instantly swaps positions with a selected target — enemy heroes, allied heroes, or various objects on the map. The ability also deals damage to the target. Swap is one of the most versatile abilities in Spelltroum. It can be used offensively to drag enemies into danger or defensively to save teammates from certain death.
abilities.MortifierMineDescription: Active Ability. Mortifier places an explosive trap beneath himself. The trap detonates when an enemy enters its trigger range or its timer expires. The explosion deals Magical Damage and slows enemies. Enemies can see the trap and attempt to avoid it, creating an interesting mind game. Blast Trap is Mortifier's signature ability and the foundation of his mage playstyle. The classic combo: place traps, wait, then Swap an enemy directly onto them.

grizzly.overview: Grizzly is one of the strongest melee fighters in Spelltroum. He combines massive Health, powerful scaling damage, self-healing, mobility, and strong dueling potential into a single hero. Grizzly excels at direct combat and becomes increasingly dangerous the longer he remains in a fight. Unlike assassins that rely on burst damage or mages that attack from a distance, Grizzly wants to get close to his target and stay there. Because of his survivability and straightforward abilities, Grizzly is often considered one of the easiest heroes to learn, while still remaining extremely effective at higher levels of play.
grizzly.playstyle: His gameplay revolves around sticking to a target and continuously attacking. The longer Grizzly attacks the same enemy, the more damage he deals. Combined with his bonus Health and healing from Bear Leap, this makes him one of the strongest heroes in direct one-on-one combat. Unlike many melee heroes, Grizzly also has strong mobility thanks to Bear Leap, allowing him to quickly close the gap against ranged opponents.
weaknesses: Relies on staying close to enemies
Can struggle against highly mobile heroes
Requires successful engages to be effective
tips: Focus on one target whenever possible.
Don't switch targets unnecessarily.
Use Bear Leap aggressively when enemies attempt to escape.
Save Snack Time for critical moments.
Let enemies underestimate your survivability.
Build enough durability to stay in fights longer.
Remember that Grizzly becomes stronger the longer combat lasts.

synergies.viverna: Viverna and Grizzly complement each other very well. Grizzly serves as the frontline while Viverna attacks from a safe distance. Enemies forced to deal with Grizzly's pressure often become easy targets for Viverna's poison and burst damage.
synergies.freezi: Freezi's Damage Aura greatly increases Grizzly's damage output. Her stun also gives Grizzly additional time to reach targets and begin stacking his Basic Attack damage. Together they create a powerful combination of crowd control and sustained damage.
synergies.death-slayer: Death Slayer's Lifesteal Aura provides even more sustain for Grizzly. The combination of Grizzly's large Health pool and additional Lifesteal can make him extremely difficult to bring down.
synergies.spiritmonger: Spiritmonger's Velocity helps Grizzly overcome one of his biggest weaknesses: mobility. The extra Movement Speed allows him to stay on targets more consistently and secure kills more reliably.

counters.death-slayer: Death Slayer is one of Grizzly's toughest opponents. Both heroes excel in direct combat, but Death Slayer's Lifesteal and stun can make extended fights extremely difficult. In many situations, Death Slayer can out-sustain Grizzly during prolonged engagements.
counters.stonewarden: Stonewarden's durability creates major problems for Grizzly. His armor, defensive abilities, and damage reflection make it difficult for Grizzly to win trades efficiently. Stonewarden can also use his wall to disrupt Grizzly's movement and positioning.

faq.q: Is Grizzly a tank or a carry?
faq.a: Both. Grizzly is naturally tanky but can also deal enormous damage during extended fights.

faq.q: How does Grizzly's Basic Attack work?
faq.a: Each consecutive hit against the same target increases the damage of future attacks.
Yes. Bear Leap deals damage and restores Health at the same time.
Yes. His abilities are straightforward, easy to understand, and effective in most situations.
Heroes that can continuously kite, slow, or avoid him are usually his toughest opponents.

faq.q: Which heroes work best with Grizzly?
faq.a: Viverna, Freezi, Death Slayer, and Spiritmonger all provide strong synergy with Grizzly.
Death Slayer, Stonewarden, and highly mobile ranged heroes are generally the most difficult matchups.

faq.q: Is Grizzly strong in late game?
faq.a: Absolutely. Once Grizzly has enough items and can stay on a target, his damage becomes extremely threatening.

abilities.UrsaAutoattackDescription: Basic Attack. Grizzly's attacks apply a stacking debuff to the target. Each consecutive attack against the same enemy deals increased damage. The longer Grizzly remains on a target, the more dangerous he becomes. This makes him exceptionally strong against tanks, bosses, high-health heroes, and enemies forced into extended fights. If an opponent cannot escape, Grizzly's damage quickly becomes overwhelming.
abilities.UrsaPowerDescription: Active Ability. Grizzly temporarily gains a large amount of bonus Health. This ability dramatically increases his survivability and allows him to stay in fights much longer than most heroes. Can be used before engaging, during combat, or as an emergency survival tool. Many Grizzly players activate it when their Health becomes low, making enemies believe they are about to secure a kill before suddenly facing a much larger Health pool.
abilities.UrsaBlinkDescription: Active Ability. Grizzly instantly leaps toward a nearby target. When Bear Leap connects, the target takes damage and is slowed, while Grizzly restores Health. This ability allows Grizzly to chase fleeing enemies, initiate fights, heal during combat, and stay on top of ranged heroes. A properly timed Bear Leap often determines whether Grizzly secures a kill or allows an enemy to escape.

faerie.overview: Faerie is a highly mobile magic hero that can be played as either an aggressive spell-casting carry or a supportive healer. Unlike most mages, Faerie can both deal significant magic damage and restore Health to allies. This flexibility allows her to adapt to many different team compositions and playstyles. She excels at hit-and-run combat, quickly diving into fights, dealing burst damage, and escaping before enemies can retaliate. Players who enjoy mobility, spell combos, and supporting teammates will find Faerie one of the most rewarding heroes in Spelltroum.
faerie.playstyle: Faerie is all about movement, positioning, and timing. Unlike frontline heroes, she constantly moves around the battlefield, looking for opportunities to strike before repositioning. She can be played as a Mage Carry (Spell Power + Magical Critical Chance for burst damage) or as a Support Mage (survivability and utility to keep teammates alive).
strengths: Can function as both carry and support
weaknesses: Vulnerable when caught by crowd control
Can struggle against heroes with strong anti-magic defenses
tips: Use Flying Sphere aggressively, but always plan your escape route.
Heal frontline allies whenever possible.
Combine Flying Sphere and Feyburst for maximum burst damage.
Don't stay in direct combat for too long.
Focus on mobility and positioning.
Save Silence for key enemy heroes in late-game fights.
Use your movement advantage to control engagements.

synergies.grizzly: Grizzly and Faerie form an excellent frontline-backline combination. Grizzly absorbs damage and forces enemies into fights while Faerie supports him from a safe distance. Her healing allows Grizzly to stay alive even longer, while Grizzly creates space for Faerie to cast spells safely. Together they create a very durable and difficult-to-kill team.
synergies.viverna: Viverna and Faerie are one of the strongest magic-based combinations in the game. Both heroes deal primarily magic damage, have excellent mobility, and prefer hit-and-run engagements. When combined with items that reduce enemy Magic Resistance, they can quickly overwhelm opponents with continuous magical pressure.
synergies.spiritmonger: Spiritmonger's Velocity provides additional mobility, allowing both heroes to move around the battlefield rapidly. Together they create a highly mobile composition that can engage, disengage, and reposition better than most teams.

counters.grizzly: Grizzly is one of the most dangerous opponents for Faerie. His large Health pool makes him difficult to burst down with spells. Even worse, Bear Leap allows him to quickly close the distance after Faerie attempts to escape. If Grizzly reaches her, surviving becomes much more difficult.
counters.freezi: Freezi's Frost Strike can stun Faerie for up to 2 seconds. Because Faerie is naturally fragile, getting caught by a fully upgraded stun can often lead to a quick defeat. Against Freezi, positioning and movement become extremely important.
counters.shadow-fury: Shadow Fury's Orb Shield is a major problem for Faerie. The shield can completely absorb Faerie's burst combo, wasting much of her damage. After surviving the combo, Shadow Fury can retaliate with devastating physical attacks. This matchup often requires patience and careful timing.

faq.q: Is Faerie a support or a carry?
faq.a: Both. She can be played as a burst-damage mage or as a supportive healer depending on your build and team composition.
Yes. Her Basic Attack restores Health when it hits friendly targets.

faq.q: Is Flying Sphere an escape ability?
faq.a: Yes. Flying Sphere is one of the strongest mobility tools in Spelltroum and can be used both offensively and defensively.
Yes. Her abilities and primary damage output are magic-based.
She has a moderate skill ceiling. New players can learn her quickly, but mastering her mobility and positioning takes practice.

faq.q: Which heroes work best with Faerie?
faq.a: Grizzly, Viverna, and Spiritmonger all have strong synergy with her playstyle.
Grizzly, Freezi, and Shadow Fury are among her most difficult matchups.
She is one of the few heroes that can simultaneously deal significant magic damage, heal allies, and maintain exceptional mobility.

abilities.FairyAutoattackDescription: Basic Attack. Faerie throws magical spheres that deal magic damage to enemies. Unlike most heroes, her attacks can also heal allies — if the projectile hits a friendly target, it restores Health instead of dealing damage. With enough Attack Speed, Faerie can become a surprisingly effective healer, constantly restoring Health to frontline allies while staying at a safe distance.
abilities.FairyBlinkBallDescription: Active Ability. Faerie transforms into a fast-moving magical sphere. While transformed, she becomes invulnerable, moves rapidly across the battlefield, and deals damage to enemies she passes through. Flying Sphere can be used to escape danger, engage fights, chase enemies, cross obstacles, or reposition during combat. Faerie can also cancel Flying Sphere early by using her Basic Attack, allowing skilled players to create unpredictable movement patterns.
abilities.FairySecondSpellDescription: Active Ability. Faerie releases a magical explosion around herself that damages all nearby enemies and objects. The ability has excellent synergy with Flying Sphere: fly into a group of enemies, exit Flying Sphere, instantly cast Feyburst, and reposition before enemies can react. Also extremely useful for farming bushes and clearing objectives quickly when combined with Spell Power items.

volcarn.overview: Volcarn is one of the strongest late-game carries in Spelltroum. He starts relatively weak and often needs time to farm, gather gold, and build his items. However, if left alone for too long, Volcarn can become an overwhelming threat capable of carrying entire matches. His gameplay revolves around powerful scaling, summoned illusions, and sustained combat. Unlike burst-oriented heroes, Volcarn becomes stronger the longer a fight lasts. Players who enjoy farming, scaling into the late game, and overwhelming enemies through raw damage will feel right at home with Volcarn.
volcarn.playstyle: Volcarn is a ranged carry that rewards patience and efficient farming. While many heroes rely on quick engagements, Volcarn becomes stronger the longer he survives. If enemies allow him to farm freely, he can quickly become one of the most dangerous heroes in the game.
playstyleHighlights: Win prolonged fights through overwhelming damage
weaknesses: Difficult to recover if enemies deny farm early
tips: Focus on farming during the early game.
Use Illusion frequently to maximize map pressure.
Fight near your portal whenever possible.
Don't panic when your Health drops — your Attack Speed increases.
Build toward late-game carry items.
Use illusions to force enemies into difficult decisions.
Avoid unnecessary early-game fights.
Once fully farmed, aggressively pressure objectives and enemy heroes.

synergies.druid: Druid is one of Volcarn's best partners. Druid provides movement speed through his passive aura, allowing Volcarn to move around the map more efficiently and farm faster. Later in the match, Druid's summoned creatures and Volcarn's illusions can overwhelm opponents through sheer numbers. Together they create a powerful summon-based composition.
synergies.spiritmonger: Spiritmonger's Velocity grants additional movement speed. This allows Volcarn to rotate faster, farm more efficiently, and reposition during fights. The extra mobility helps Volcarn reach his late-game power spikes sooner.
synergies.death-slayer: Death Slayer provides Lifesteal Aura, which is incredibly valuable for Volcarn. Volcarn already excels in extended fights, and additional Lifesteal makes him even harder to bring down. Death Slayer can also protect Volcarn with crowd control while Volcarn focuses on farming and scaling.

faq.a: Yes. Volcarn is one of the strongest late-game carry heroes in Spelltroum.
Yes. Illusions inherit Volcarn's items and most combat bonuses.

faq.q: How much damage does the Illusion deal?
faq.a: The Illusion deals 50% of Volcarn's normal damage.

faq.q: Why does Volcarn attack faster when injured?
faq.a: His Basic Attack passively increases Attack Speed based on missing Health.
Not usually. Volcarn becomes significantly stronger after acquiring items and levels.
If Volcarn has unlocked his Super Ability, his Illusions gain additional damage and copy Demon's Snack Time effects.

faq.q: Which heroes work best with Volcarn?
faq.a: Druid, Spiritmonger, and Death Slayer are among his strongest teammates.
Volcarn combines powerful scaling, self-healing, illusion gameplay, and increasing Attack Speed as his Health drops, making him one of the most dangerous late-game heroes in the game.

abilities.VolcarnBasicAttackDescription: Basic Attack. Volcarn gains increased Attack Speed based on his missing Health. The lower his Health becomes, the faster he attacks. This creates a unique risk-versus-reward playstyle — during intense fights, Volcarn often becomes more dangerous as his Health decreases. Combined with his other abilities, this allows him to output incredible sustained damage during extended battles.
abilities.VolcarnPowerUpDescription: Active Ability. Demon's Snack Time causes Volcarn's attacks to cycle through special effects. Each consecutive attack applies a different bonus: Physical Armor reduction, chance to stun, and self-healing. As the ability levels up, these effects become significantly stronger. At maximum level, Volcarn can apply substantial Armor Reduction, gain a high chance to stun, and restore significant Health during combat. Once fully upgraded, every attack contributes additional utility and damage.
abilities.VolcarnIllusionDescription: Active Ability. Volcarn creates a portal beneath himself. While standing on the portal, Volcarn is continuously healed. If he leaves the portal before it expires, the portal transforms into an Illusion of Volcarn. The illusion inherits items, Critical Strike bonuses, armor reduction effects, Attack Speed bonuses, and most combat statistics — but deals only 50% of Volcarn's damage. When Volcarn becomes fully built, even 50% of his damage can be extremely threatening.

stonewarden.overview: Stonewarden is one of the toughest heroes in Spelltroum. He specializes in durability, battlefield control, and team support. Unlike most heroes that focus on damage or mobility, Stonewarden excels at controlling space, protecting allies, and forcing enemies into unfavorable fights. His powerful Armor Aura makes both himself and nearby allies significantly harder to kill, while his Stone Wall can block paths, trap enemies, and create opportunities for his team. Stonewarden can be played as a frontline tank, a support, or a defensive bruiser that slowly overwhelms opponents through survivability and damage reflection.
stonewarden.playstyle: Stonewarden is not a hero that relies on flashy combos or mobility. He performs best when standing between enemies and his allies, forcing opponents to fight on his terms. Because of his naturally high damage and defensive tools, Stonewarden can remain relevant throughout every stage of the match.
strengths: Difficult to kill in prolonged fights
weaknesses: Can struggle against highly mobile heroes
Less explosive damage than dedicated carries
tips: Use Stone Wall to block escape routes, not just to deal damage.
Position yourself between enemies and your allies.
Take advantage of Armor Aura during team fights.
Remember that reflected damage can win trades over time.
Attack Speed items work exceptionally well with Stonewarden's high base damage.
Farm bushes whenever possible to maximize your late-game Super Ability.
Use walls to create opportunities for teammates, not only for yourself.

synergies.spiritmonger: Spiritmonger has one of the strongest combinations with Stonewarden. Stonewarden can trap enemies using Stone Wall, allowing Spiritmonger's Soul Orb to repeatedly bounce between the trapped enemy and nearby wall segments. This combination can deal enormous amounts of damage and is one of the most dangerous control-and-burst combos in the game.
synergies.druid: Stonewarden works very well with Druid. Druid can summon creeps from Stonewarden's walls, creating additional pressure and unexpected tactical opportunities. The combination of walls, summons, and battlefield control makes it difficult for enemies to move freely.
synergies.grizzly: Stonewarden and Grizzly form an incredibly durable frontline. Grizzly naturally gains massive Health through Snack Time, while Stonewarden provides additional Armor through Armor Aura. The result is a pair of heroes that become extremely difficult to bring down, excelling in direct team fights and objective control.

faq.q: Is Stonewarden a tank or a support?
faq.a: Both. He can function as a frontline tank, a defensive support, or a hybrid of the two depending on the team's needs.
Yes. Armor Aura grants bonus Armor to both Stonewarden and nearby allies.
Yes. Stone Wall can block movement, cut off escape routes, and temporarily trap opponents.
Yes. Enemies hit by the wall when it appears take damage immediately.
At higher levels, Armor Aura reflects 20% of incoming damage back to attackers.
Very. Between Armor Aura, bonus Health, and damage reflection, Stonewarden is one of the toughest heroes in Spelltroum.

faq.q: Which heroes work best with Stonewarden?
faq.a: Spiritmonger, Druid, and Grizzly all have excellent synergy with Stonewarden.
His combination of Armor Aura, damage reflection, wall control, and exceptional durability allows him to influence fights even when he isn't dealing the most damage.

abilities.GolemBasicAttackDescription: Basic Attack. Stonewarden gains bonus damage on his attacks but suffers reduced Attack Speed. He attacks slower than most heroes but each hit deals significantly more damage. Many Stonewarden players compensate with Attack Speed items, allowing him to benefit from both high damage and improved attack frequency. This makes his Basic Attack scale very well into the late game.
abilities.GolemWallDescription: Active Ability. Stonewarden creates a wall of stone directly in front of him. The wall can block enemy movement, cut off escape routes, protect allies, create chokepoints, and trap enemies. If the wall appears on top of an enemy, it deals damage immediately. Stone Walls can be destroyed by enemy attacks, but doing so often wastes valuable time and positioning. A skilled Stonewarden can completely change the flow of a fight with a well-placed wall.
abilities.GolemThirdSpellDescription: Passive Ability. Stonewarden continuously grants bonus Armor to himself and nearby allies, making his entire team significantly more durable against physical damage. At higher levels, Armor Aura gains an additional effect: 20% Damage Reflection. Whenever Stonewarden takes damage, a portion is automatically reflected back to the attacker. Attack Stonewarden and take damage yourself — or ignore him and allow him to control the fight. Either option benefits Stonewarden's team.
